description: WORKED EXAMPLE of the canonical Config & Secret Registry (RS-DOS §57.2). It demonstrates every field and every data_class, and shows how the two legacy shapes map onto this one: a SECRET sourced on Vercel (DATABASE_URL), a PII-class config, an INTERNAL public-base URL, a github-plane SECRET (CI token), a PUBLIC flag, and a local-only dev secret. Metadata ONLY — every 'example' is redacted; no field carries a value. The I-Config oracle (i-config.mjs) reads this and returns PRESENT|MISSING|INVALID|DRIFTED per key over the live planes; it never reads/prints a value for a remote-plane key and never emits a secret value.

keys.purpose: Supabase EU Postgres connection. THE build-blocking required secret AND the cause/cure of the connection-exhaustion 503 — it must be the TRANSACTION-POOLER (port 6543) URL. data_class SECRET ⇒ §54.2 trust boundary: trusted-domain only, never tree-resident.
keys.fix: Set DATABASE_URL in Vercel (Production) to the Supabase Transaction-pooler URI (port 6543), NOT the direct :5432 string.

keys.purpose: The Supabase project JWT secret the engine surface uses to verify Supabase access tokens. Absent ⇒ the engine routes 401 (fail-closed seam). Optional in dev/CI (inert seam); required to operate in prod.
keys.fix: Supabase → Settings → API → 'JWT Secret' → set as a Vercel Production env var (min 32 chars).

keys.purpose: The sender mailbox (From address) for outbound mail — a personal email address, hence data_class PII (§54.2 trust boundary applies; never copied into a non-prod env). OPTIONAL: with the Gmail vars unset the mailer no-ops.
keys.fix: OPTIONAL (real email send). Set GMAIL_SENDER to the From mailbox alongside the other GMAIL_* / GOOGLE_OAUTH_* vars.

keys.purpose: The deployed base URL stamped into invoice/notification email links. Not a secret (INTERNAL) — its value is readable on the plane and can be format-validated.
keys.fix: Set PUBLIC_BASE_URL as a Vercel Production env var to the deployed prod base.

keys.purpose: Vercel access token the GitHub deploy workflow uses and that the oracle uses (read-only) to verify prod env state. Lives in GitHub Actions secrets — checked presence-only via `gh secret list` (names, never values).
keys.fix: GitHub → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret: VERCEL_TOKEN.

keys.purpose: Master switch for the discovery/research lane. DEFAULT OFF — an unset flag is reported (not guessed). PUBLIC: a non-secret operational toggle.
keys.fix: OPTIONAL. Set DISCOVERY_ENABLED=1 to enable the discovery pipeline; leave unset to keep it inert.

keys.purpose: A local-only developer secret (sourced from a gitignored .env, inside the §54.2 trust boundary). env_scope is LOCAL ONLY — if this key ever appears on the Vercel/prod plane the oracle reports DRIFTED (a SECRET escaped its scope).
keys.fix: Set DEV_SEED_TOKEN in your local .env (min 16 chars). It must NEVER be promoted to dev/staging/prod planes.

keys.purpose: DEDICATED node-controlled HS256 signing key for SERVICE (machine) tokens — the decoupling seam. Today the engine verifies ALL tokens (user + service) with AUTH_JWT_SECRET (the Supabase LEGACY symmetric JWT secret); Supabase is migrating to asymmetric signing keys, so that symmetric secret is a long-term liability. This key lives ONLY in the vault (origin=generated), is rotated on OUR schedule (secret-resolver seed --from generated), and is intended as a SECOND HS256 verifier key the engine accepts for service tokens — making machine automation fully independent of Supabase user-key lifecycle. Follow-on ENGINE change required to honor it (design note only; not implemented here).
keys.example: <32+ char generated signing key — vault-only, never on a platform>
keys.fix: Generate + store in the vault: node infra/secret-resolver.mjs seed SERVICE_SIGNING_KEY --env prod --from generated. Rotate the same way. ENGINE follow-on: accept SERVICE_SIGNING_KEY as a second HS256 key for aud=service tokens (dual-verify during overlap).

keys.purpose: The MINTED HS256 engine service token the service consumer presents to the downstream API (bounded TTL, e.g. 30 days). Minted from SERVICE_SIGNING_KEY (preferred) or AUTH_JWT_SECRET (today) and stored in the vault. AUTO-RENEWED before expiry by scripts/renew-service-token.mjs (a launchd timer) — never manually re-issued. Rotation-safe: on a 401 from the engine (root rotated at origin) the renewer re-syncs the root and re-mints, or fails closed + alerts.
keys.fix: Mint + store once: node scripts/renew-service-token.mjs --once. Then enable the launchd auto-renew timer (docs/BOOTSTRAP-secret-resolver.md §7).
